welcome.subtitle: In the new digital age, data is the raw material that drives innovation, efficiency, and business profitability.
welcome.quote_part1: Today, organizations generate more data than they can manage.
welcome.quote_part2: At DataSign we manage its life cycle with precision, ensuring operational continuity and strengthening decision-making.

about.title: About Us
about.description: At DataSign we help organizations better manage, protect, and leverage their data. We integrate infrastructure, cyber resilience, and information governance solutions so businesses can operate with security, efficiency, and continuity. Our approach is practical, transparent, and results-oriented.
about.missionContent: Help organizations better manage, protect, and leverage their data through reliable, efficient, and results-oriented technological solutions.
about.visionContent: Be a reference in data management and protection, driving the growth and competitiveness of our clients through clear, useful, and impactful technology.
about.valuesContent: Innovation, transparency, commitment, and collaboration with our clients.

innovation.title: Innovation
innovation.content: We constantly seek new ways to improve, adopting technologies and practices that provide real value to our clients.

quality.title: Quality
quality.content: We care about every detail of what we do to offer stable, secure, and high-performance solutions.

service.title: Service
service.content: We support our clients with close, professional, and solution-oriented attention.

honesty.title: Honesty
honesty.content: We act with transparency, always speaking the truth and prioritizing correct and responsible decisions.

growth.title: Sustainable Growth
growth.content: We build lasting relationships and solutions, thinking long-term for clients, employees, and partners.

about.whyChooseUsContent: We combine technical expertise with business understanding to offer solutions that truly drive your business forward.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.description: In compliance with the Federal Law on the Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties, DataSign S.A. de C.V., with RFC DAT2110192V8 and address at Av. Acueducto 6075 A Local 4, Col. Puerta de Hierro, C.P. 45116, Zapopan, Jalisco, is responsible for collecting your personal data, its use, and protection.

sections.data_security: 5. Security and Protection of Personal Data

personal_data: At DataSign, we collect personal information that you provide directly, including but not limited to:
We may also collect non-personal information through website analytics tools, such as cookies.
data_purposes: We collect your personal data to provide our services and products, including but not limited to:
Custom software development for companies and organizations.
IT consulting services for advice and support in the use of information technologies.
Support and maintenance of computer systems.
Cloud services for data storage and management.
Cybersecurity for network and system protection and data recovery in case of security incidents.
Website and mobile application development for companies and organizations.
System integration to improve efficiency and business process automation.
Data analysis for informed and strategic decision-making.
data_sharing: At DataSign, we do not share your personal data with third parties, unless necessary to provide our services and products or when required by law.
arco_rights: You have the right to access, rectify, cancel, or oppose the processing of your personal data, as well as to revoke the consent you have granted for its use.
To exercise these rights, please contact us through the following means:
In writing, by sending a request addressed to the DataSign Privacy Department, at Av. Acueducto 6075 A Local 4, Col. Puerta de Hierro, C.P. 45116, Zapopan, Jalisco, Mexico.
By email, by sending a message to privacidad@datasign.mx.

The procedure for exercising ARCO rights and revoking consent, as well as the time frames, requirements, and means to make the request, will be governed by the Federal Law on the Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties and its Regulations.
Means to exercise ARCO rights and revoke consent:

The procedure for exercising ARCO rights and revoking consent, as well as the deadlines, requirements, and means for submitting the request, will be governed by the provisions of the Federal Law on the Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties and its Regulations.
data_security: At DataSign, we implement physical, technical, and administrative security measures to protect your personal data and prevent its loss, misuse, unauthorized access, or disclosure.
We also have security and privacy policies and procedures to ensure the protection of your personal data.
policy_changes: We reserve the right to modify or update this privacy notice at any time to comply with legislative or jurisprudential developments, internal policies, new requirements for the provision or offering of our services or products, and market practices.
In case of any changes to this privacy notice, we will communicate them through our website https://www.datasign.mx, by email, or any other means we deem appropriate.
If you do not express your opposition to the processing of your personal data according to the terms and conditions of this notice, it will be understood that you have given your tacit consent.
This privacy notice may be modified at any time, and any change will be published on our website.

contact.address: Av. Acueducto 6075 A Local 4, Col. Puerta de Hierro, C.P. 45116, Zapopan, Jalisco, Mexico

introduction.paragraph1: This Code of Conduct defines the minimum standards of business behavior and commercial practices under which DATASIGN operates with each of our clients and business prospects.
introduction.paragraph2: DATASIGN may change this Code of Conduct at any time by publishing a revised version on its website (https://datasign.mx).

financial_integrity.title: Financial Integrity and Accounting
financial_integrity.content: Accurate and reliable financial records are critically important. None of our employees may engage in any action that could result in the transmission of false or inaccurate financial information to any of our suppliers or clients. All submissions we make to suppliers or clients (such as orders, sales reports, special offer requests, rebates, and reimbursement claims) must be complete and accurate.

government_clients.title: Working with Government Clients
government_clients.content: Each of our employees must understand and comply with all laws, rules, and regulations, including procurement regulations and contract clauses governing the acquisition of goods and services by government entities with which DATASIGN or any of our business partners (including suppliers or resellers) directly or indirectly conduct business. This includes federal, state, and local entities, as well as government-owned or controlled organizations or those subject to public procurement laws ("government clients"). Employees should be aware that activities appropriate with non-government clients may be inappropriate or even illegal when dealing with government clients.

no_illicit_payments.title: No Illicit Payments
content: We require our employees to comply at all times with all applicable local and foreign anti-bribery and anti-corruption laws. Practices acceptable in the private sector may be completely unacceptable when dealing with government officials and may even violate applicable laws and regulations.

When working with government officials or anyone acting on behalf of a government, employees must be aware of these restrictions. They must not, directly or indirectly, make or offer bribes or other payments of money or items of value—including business services—to any person, including government officials, employees, representatives, political parties, candidates, public or international organizations, or any third party, for the purpose of unlawfully obtaining or retaining business related to products or services provided or resold by DATASIGN.

This includes giving money or services to any third party when there is reason to believe it will be passed to someone involved in a business decision-making process to improperly influence that decision. Even when permitted by applicable laws and regulations, any expenses related to travel or services provided to state entities must be reasonable, tied to legitimate product demonstrations, and not intended to unlawfully obtain or retain business related to DATASIGN products or services.

All employees must ensure that any business services provided to private, commercial, or government-owned clients comply with applicable laws and regulations, occur in the normal course of business, and cannot reasonably be interpreted as bribes or improper incentives.
When dealing with others, including DATASIGN business partners, employees must exercise due diligence to identify any warning signs of potential issues and strictly adhere to this Code of Conduct.

competition_laws.title: Antitrust and Competition Laws
content: DATASIGN must fully comply with all applicable antitrust and competition laws and regulations.

No DATASIGN employee or business partner may engage in or attempt any of the following: (1) fixing or controlling prices in a purchasing process; (2) boycotting suppliers or customers; (3) dividing or allocating markets or clients; or (4) coordinating bids with competitors.

fair_competition.title: Fair Competition
fair_competition.content: DATASIGN expects all employees involved in the sale of DATASIGN products and services to ensure that all statements and communications to clients are accurate, complete, and truthful. Likewise, employees must not make unauthorized commitments on behalf of DATASIGN or its clients, or improperly involve DATASIGN in client or third-party disputes. Employees must also avoid defaming or disparaging DATASIGN, its business partners, competitors, or clients.

securities_laws.title: Compliance with Securities and Insider Trading Laws
content: All DATASIGN employees must fully comply with applicable securities and insider trading laws governing transactions in DATASIGN securities. These securities include common stock, bonds, options, futures, and other financial instruments.

If any employee possesses or has access to material non-public information obtained through their work with DATASIGN or our clients, they must use that information only for its intended purpose and must not use it to trade securities. These restrictions also apply to family members and friends.

intellectual_property.title: Intellectual Property
intellectual_property.content: Each employee is responsible for protecting the intellectual property rights of DATASIGN and our clients. A key part of this protection is maintaining the confidentiality of proprietary and confidential information. Employees must not reproduce software, documentation, or other copyrighted materials unless properly authorized to do so. Data privacy requirements must always be observed. When selling directly to an end user, employees must ensure that proper licensing and product terms are provided in a legally binding form before the sale is finalized.

respect_dignity.title: Respect and Dignity
respect_dignity.content: DATASIGN is committed to providing its employees with a work environment free from coercion, discrimination, and harassment.

social_media.title: Social Media
social_media.content: Any DATASIGN employee using social media (such as any form of online posting or discussion, including blogs, wikis, file sharing, and user-generated video or audio) must comply with this Code of Conduct and must not disclose confidential information belonging to DATASIGN or its business partners.

marketing.title: Working with Marketing Partners
marketing.content: DATASIGN requires its business partners to comply with this Code of Conduct and to regularly review DATASIGN's website for any updates or changes to this policy.

unauthorized_sources.title: Purchasing from Unauthorized Sources
unauthorized_sources.content: Purchasing products from unauthorized sources is strictly prohibited. Such actions jeopardize our commitment to providing clients with the highest levels of quality and business value. It also poses significant risks to potential customers, as there is no guarantee that these products are free from defects or alterations—or that they are genuine—which can affect warranty claims and customer satisfaction.

training.title: Integrity Training, Certification, and Renewal
training.content: All DATASIGN employees must complete DATASIGN's integrity training and certify their compliance with this Code of Conduct.

compliance.title: Compliance
compliance.content: Any violation of this Code of Conduct by employees or business partners constitutes grounds for the immediate termination of employment or business relationships with DATASIGN, including all related contracts.

reporting_violations.title: Reporting Violations
reporting_violations.content: If any DATASIGN employee or business partner becomes aware of any illegal or unethical situation involving the sale of DATASIGN products or services, they must immediately report it by emailing compliance@datasign.mx and providing any information related to the incident or situation.

infra.title: Mission-Critical IT Infrastructure
infra.subtitle: We design and operate network, compute, and storage infrastructure for mission-critical environments
infra.description: We implement networks, x86 compute platforms, and IBM Power Systems (AIX and Linux), as well as storage and data protection systems designed to ensure continuity, high performance, and security in enterprise environments.

services.title: Specialized Services
services.subtitle: Experts in technologies that enable secure and fast services
services.description: A team of specialists in various technologies that enable secure and fast services.

saas.title: SaaS Orchestration
saas.subtitle: Full control, protection, and management of your data in Microsoft 365, Google, and other SaaS platforms.
saas.description: We implement solutions to migrate, protect, and govern cloud data, integrating leading technologies that ensure continuity, compliance, and total visibility.
points: Backup and recovery for SaaS applications
Operational continuity and protection against loss or corruption
Integrated technology: AvePoint · Varonis · Datto (Kaseya) · Nimboxe

archive.title: Digital Archive
archive.subtitle: Secure, organized, and efficient enterprise archiving.
archive.description: Digital Archive organizes and moves large volumes of data to the appropriate storage—disk, tape, or cloud—without interrupting operations. It lets you analyze your data, migrate it intelligently, and keep it protected and ready for future needs, including analytics and AI.
points: Data ready for analytics and AI.

cyber.title: CyberResilience
cyber.subtitle: Comprehensive protection to keep your data safe and your operations running
cyber.description: Our solutions ensure that critical information remains available and recoverable even in the face of attacks or incidents. We combine immutable storage, isolated backups, and rapid recovery to guarantee continuity.
points: Advanced data protection: encryption, isolation, and immutability
Fast and reliable recovery to restore operations
Technology ecosystem: Veeam, ObjectFirst, Spectra Logic, Nimboxe
